Title: Tree architecture: A strigolactone-deficient mutant reveals a connection between branching order and auxin gradient along the tree stem
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: kanttarelli mutant; WT clone V5834 used for transgenics
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Shoot branching inhibition; maintenance of tree-like architecture and plant height
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Carotenoid-derived strigolactone branch downstream of MAX3/MAX4-derived carlactone oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step supported for SL production in birch; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes strigolactone precursor carlactone into downstream carlactonoid intermediates including carlactonoic acid and 18-hydroxycarlactonoic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms downstream intermediates leading to target strigolactones
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss or downregulation of BpMAX1 strongly reduces strigolactone levels and promotes bushy architecture with increased second-order branching, shorter stature, and smaller branch angles
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Natural loss-of-function mutant identification; RNAi knockdown in birch; LC-MS/MS strigolactone profiling; O. minor germination bioassay; Arabidopsis max1 complementation; qRT-PCR; promoter-GUS
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in shoot vasculature and axillary buds; prominent in xylem ray parenchyma; transcript peaks in xylem tissue
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in shoot vasculature and axillary buds; prominent in xylem ray parenchyma; transcript peaks in xylem tissue
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pBpMAX1 native promoter (promoter-GUS and Arabidopsis complementation); RNAi promoter NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; hydroponic culture for root exudate SL profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for breeding or editing tree architecture: maintaining BpMAX1 activity favors taller timber-type trees with fewer higher-order branches, whereas reducing activity may be useful for compact ornamental or horticultural forms; caution due to shorter stature and altered branch angles
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced plant height; compact form; increased higher-order branching may reduce timber quality
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BpMAX1 is the only AtMAX1 ortholog in the silver birch genome; pBpMAX1::BpMAX1 rescued Arabidopsis max1 branching and height phenotypes; birch also appears to contain an unidentified noncanonical strigolactone
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (published birch transcriptome resource cited; accession not provided in excerpt); metabolite profiling by LC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A naturally occurring early stop codon in BpMAX1 was identified in the bushy birch variant kanttarelli. 2. RNAi suppression of BpMAX1 phenocopied kanttarelli and markedly reduced strigolactone output. 3. Reduced BpMAX1 function caused shorter stems, increased second-order branching, and more compact architecture, linking strigolactone biosynthesis to tree-versus-bush form.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Upstream carotenoid cleavage step required for strigolactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis only in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as another birch ortholog of SL biosynthetic genes; not experimentally tested as core causal gene in Results
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Published birch RNA-seq resource cited; accession not provided
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BpMAX3 is expressed specifically in xylem tissue in birch. 2. It provides contextual support for xylem-associated SL biosynthesis. 3. It was not functionally dissected as a primary causal gene in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes carotenoid-derived conversion leading to carlactone formation in strigolactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis only in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as another birch ortholog of SL biosynthetic genes; not functionally validated as core causal gene in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Published birch RNA-seq resource cited; accession not provided
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BpMAX4 is expressed specifically in xylem tissue in birch. 2. It supports the idea that SL biosynthesis may occur in xylem-associated tissues. 3. It was not a primary experimental target in this study.
